2605.02443.title: HalluScan: A Systematic Benchmark for Detecting and Mitigating Hallucinations in Instruction-Following LLMs
2605.02443.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated remarkable capabilities across diverse natural language processing tasks, yet they remain susceptible to hallucinations -- generating content that is factually incorrect, unfaithful to provided context, or misaligned with user instructions. We present HalluScan, a comprehensive benchmark framework that systematically evaluates hallucination detection and mitigation across 72 configurations spanning 6 detection methods, 4 open-weight model families, and 3 diverse domains. We introduce three key contributions: (1) HalluScore, a novel composite metric that achieves a Pearson correlation of r = 0.41 with human expert judgments; (2) Adaptive Detection Routing (ADR), an intelligent routing algorithm achieving 2.0x cost reduction with only 0.1% AUROC degradation; and (3) systematic error cascade decomposition revealing substantial variation in hallucination error types across domains. Our experiments reveal that NLI Verification achieves the highest overall AUROC of 0.88, while RAV achieves the second-highest AUROC of 0.66.
2605.02443.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在各種自然語言處理任務中展現了卓越的能力，但它們仍然容易出現幻覺——生成事實上不正確、與提供的上下文不一致或與用戶指示不符的內容。我們提出了HalluScan，一個全面的基準框架，系統性地評估72個配置中的幻覺檢測和緩解，涵蓋6種檢測方法、4個開放權重模型系列和3個不同領域。我們介紹了三個主要貢獻：（1）HalluScore，一種新穎的綜合指標，與人類專家的評判達到Pearson相關係數r = 0.41；（2）自適應檢測路由（ADR），一種智能路由算法，實現了2.0倍的成本降低，僅以0.1%的AUROC降級；以及（3）系統性錯誤級聯分解，揭示了不同領域中幻覺錯誤類型的顯著變化。我們的實驗顯示，NLI驗證達到了最高的整體AUROC 0.88，而RAV則達到了第二高的AUROC 0.66。
2605.02443.author: Ahmed Cherif et.al.
